pi_items.source: Verified in verifier.py line 35 default value matches task.json claim. Also appears in task description as required recipient.

Verified by file count in benchmarks/cua_world/environments/bluemail_env/assets/emails/ham/: 50 ham_*.eml files found. setup_task.sh loads all ham_*.eml files into inbox. verifier.py line 36 default matches.

Verified in verifier.py line 37 where min_folders defaults to 2. Line 79 checks if custom_folder_count >= min_folders for full 25 points. README.md line 30 also confirms '2+ custom IMAP folders exist after task'.

Verified in verifier.py line 38 where min_moved defaults to 15. Line 98 checks if inbox_count < (initial_inbox - min_moved) for full 25 points. README.md line 31 states 'Inbox has fewer than 35 emails (15+ moved from baseline of 50)'.

privileged_info_summary: The task loads 50 real ham emails from the Apache SpamAssassin public corpus into an IMAP inbox. These emails originate from authentic mailing lists including Irish Linux Users Group (ILUG, 15 emails), SpamAssassin talk and development lists, the zzzzteana digest list, exmh-workers, and others. The verifier expects the agent to create 2+ custom IMAP folders, move at least 15 emails from the inbox to these folders, and draft/send a summary email to devops-team@techcorp.org. Success requires organizing the inbox (reducing it to fewer than 35 emails) while identifying which mailing lists were present in the dataset.
